channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Bota Bio Rebrands as Enhe Technology and Launches AI Biomanufacturing Platform SAION AI
item.description: Bota Bio rebrands as Enhe Technology, unveiling SAION AI—the world's first Physical AI platform for biomanufacturing—achieving SOTA performance across four life science AI benchmarks.

item.title: Tencent Begins Testing QClaw, Enabling One-Click Deployment of OpenClaw With WeChat and QQ Integration
item.description: Tencent launches internal testing for QClaw, a one-click OpenClaw deployment package that integrates with WeChat and QQ, allowing users to remotely control AI agents through chat interfaces.

item.title: WUWENAI Launches Physical AI Data Infrastructure Platform “Wuyin” in Zhejiang
item.description: WUWENAI unveils Wuyin, the industry's first physical AI data foundation platform in Deqing, Zhejiang, featuring over 1,000 TB of data and 10,000 hours of open-sourced high-quality datasets.

item.title: Tencent Open-Sources World Model Reinforcement Learning Framework WorldCompass
item.description: Tencent's Hunyuan team open-sources WorldCompass, a reinforcement learning post-training framework for world models that boosts complex action execution accuracy by 35 percentage points.

item.title: PsiBot Raises $280M to Accelerate Embodied AI Data Collection and Logistics Deployment
item.description: PsiBot completes a $280 million angel and Pre-Series A financing round backed by state-backed funds and strategic investors, aiming to scale embodied AI data infrastructure and expand logistics deployments.

item.title: Threat Actors Mass-Scan Salesforce Experience Cloud via Modified AuraInspector Tool
item.description: Salesforce has warned of an increase in threat actor activity that's aimed at exploiting misconfigurations in publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites by making use of a customized version of an open-source tool called AuraInspector. The activity, per the company, involves the exploitation of...

item.title: Cylake Raises $45 Million to Secure Organizations Barred From Cloud
item.description: The company, founded by Palo Alto Networks’ Nir Zuk, has developed a platform that focuses on data sovereignty. The post Cylake Raises $45 Million to Secure Organizations Barred From Cloud appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: NVIDIA's AI Engineers: Agent Inference at Planetary Scale and "Speed of Light" — Nader Khalil (Brev), Kyle Kranen (Dynamo)
item.description: NVIDIA welcomes AI Engineers with a special pre GTC episode!

item.title: CISA Flags SolarWinds, Ivanti, and Workspace One Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added three security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability list is as follows - CVE-2021-22054 (CVSS score: 7.5) - A server-side request...

item.title: Conversational Demand Response: Bidirectional Aggregator-Prosumer Coordination through Agentic AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.06217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Residential demand response depends on sustained prosumer participation, yet existing coordination is either fully automated, or limited to one-way dispatch signals and price alerts that offer little possibility for informed decision-making. This...

item.title: Artificial Intelligence for Climate Adaptation: Reinforcement Learning for Climate Change-Resilient Transport
item.description: arXiv:2603.06278v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Climate change is expected to intensify rainfall and, consequently, pluvial flooding, leading to increased disruptions in urban transportation systems over the coming decades. Designing effective adaptation strategies is challenging due to the...

item.title: The EpisTwin: A Knowledge Graph-Grounded Neuro-Symbolic Architecture for Personal AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.06290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Personal Artificial Intelligence is currently hindered by the fragmentation of user data across isolated silos. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation offers a partial remedy, its reliance on unstructured vector similarity fails to capture the latent...

item.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
item.description: arXiv:2603.06333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recursive self-improvement is moving from theory to practice: modern systems can critique, revise, and evaluate their own outputs, yet iterative self-modification risks subtle alignment drift. We introduce SAHOO, a practical framework to monitor and...

item.title: Talk Freely, Execute Strictly: Schema-Gated Agentic AI for Flexible and Reproducible Scientific Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2603.06394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can now translate a researcher's plain-language goal into executable computation, yet scientific workflows demand determinism, provenance, and governance that are difficult to guarantee when an LLM decides what runs....

item.title: vLLM Hook v0: A Plug-in for Programming Model Internals on vLLM
item.description: arXiv:2603.06588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern artificial intelligence (AI) models are deployed on inference engines to optimize runtime efficiency and resource allocation, particularly for transformer-based large language models (LLMs). The vLLM project is a major open-source library to...

item.title: How Attention Sinks Emerge in Large Language Models: An Interpretability Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.06591v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often allocate disproportionate attention to specific tokens, a phenomenon commonly referred to as the attention sink. While such sinks are generally considered detrimental, prior studies have identified a notable...

item.title: FuzzingRL: Reinforcement Fuzz-Testing for Revealing VLM Failures
item.description: arXiv:2603.06600v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision Language Models (VLMs) are prone to errors, and identifying where these errors occur is critical for ensuring the reliability and safety of AI systems. In this paper, we propose an approach that automatically generates questions designed to...

item.title: Switchable Activation Networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.06601v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep neural networks, and more recently large-scale generative models such as large language models (LLMs) and large vision-action models (LVAs), achieve remarkable performance across diverse domains, yet their prohibitive computational cost hinders...

item.title: Khatri-Rao Clustering for Data Summarization
item.description: arXiv:2603.06602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As datasets continue to grow in size and complexity, finding succinct yet accurate data summaries poses a key challenge. Centroid-based clustering, a widely adopted approach to address this challenge, finds informative summaries of datasets in terms...

item.title: ARC-AGI-2 Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2603.06590v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) is designed to assess generalization beyond pattern matching, requiring models to infer symbolic rules from very few examples. In this work, we present a transformer-based system that advances ARC performance...

item.title: Hierarchical Latent Structures in Data Generation Process Unify Mechanistic Phenomena across Scale
item.description: arXiv:2603.06592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Contemporary studies have uncovered many puzzling phenomena in the neural information processing of Transformer-based language models. Building a robust, unified understanding of these phenomena requires disassembling a model within the scope of its...

item.title: Hierarchical Embedding Fusion for Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.06593v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented code generation often conditions the decoder on large retrieved code snippets. This ties online inference cost to repository size and introduces noise from long contexts. We present Hierarchical Embedding Fusion (HEF), a two-stage...

item.title: A Coin Flip for Safety: LLM Judges Fail to Reliably Measure Adversarial Robustness
item.description: arXiv:2603.06594v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated \enquote{LLM-as-a-Judge} frameworks have become the de facto standard for scalable evaluation across natural language processing. For instance, in safety evaluation, these judges are relied upon to evaluate harmfulness in order to benchmark...

item.title: Rethinking Personalization in Large Language Models at the Token Level
item.description: arXiv:2603.06595v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With large language models (LLMs) now performing strongly across diverse tasks, there is growing demand for them to personalize outputs for individual users. Personalization is typically framed as an additional layer on top of a base NLP task,...

item.title: [AINews] Autoresearch: Sparks of Recursive Self Improvement
item.description: AGI takes another small step forward.

item.title: Deep Research requests are erroring for LATAM users
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Deep Research (Operational)

item.title: Granite 4.0 1B Speech: Compact, Multilingual, and Built for the Edge
Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts. The package, named "@openclaw-ai/openclawai," was uploaded to the registry by a user named...

item.title: StreamReady: Learning What to Answer and When in Long Streaming Videos
item.description: Streaming video understanding often involves time-sensitive scenarios where models need to answer exactly when the supporting visual evidence appears: answering before the evidence reflects speculation, answering after it has passed reduces real-time utility. To capture this behavior, we introduce...

item.title: PRISM: Streaming Human Motion Generation with Per-Joint Latent Decomposition
item.description: Text-to-motion generation has advanced rapidly, yet two challenges persist. First, existing motion autoencoders compress each frame into a single monolithic latent vector, entangling trajectory and per-joint rotations in an unstructured representation that downstream generators struggle to model...

item.title: Elevated conversation errors for Enterprise and Edu users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Mar 9, 15:56 UTCUpdate - We are experiencing latency on the API and UI endpoints. We are working to resolve the issue.Mar 9, 15:50 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Webhooks

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 42 Deals Announced in February 2026
item.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Check Point, Booz Allen, Proofpoint, Sophos, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 42 Deals Announced in February 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. “Anyone wanna host a get together in SF and pull this up on a 100 inch TV?”  The author of that post on X was referring to an online intelligence dashboard...

item.title: Codex CLI unresponsive
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components Codex (Degraded performance)

item.title: UNC4899 Breached Crypto Firm After Developer AirDropped Trojanized File to Work Device
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as UNC4899 is suspected to be behind a sophisticated cloud compromise campaign targeting a cryptocurrency organization in 2025 to steal millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The activity has been attributed with moderate confidence to the state-sponsored...

item.title: The usability imperative for securing digital asset devices
item.description: When Tony Fadell started working on the iPod, usability often trumped security. The result was an iterative process. Every time someone would find a security weakness or a way to hack the device, the development group would iterate to add measures and fix the issues. Yet, flaws would frequently be...

item.title: The Download: murky AI surveillance laws, and the White House cracks down on defiant labs
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI? The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
item.description: Another week in cybersecurity. Another week of "you've got to be kidding me." Attackers were busy. Defenders were busy. And somewhere in the middle, a whole lot of people had a very bad Monday morning. That's kind of just how it goes now. The good news? There were some actual wins this week. Real...

item.title: Do Language Models Know Theo Has a Wife? Investigating the Proviso Problem
item.description: We investigate how language models handle the proviso problem, an unresolved issue in pragmatics where presuppositions in conditional sentences diverge between theoretical and human interpretations. We reformulate this phenomenon as a Natural Language Inference task and introduce a diagnostic...

item.title: ClickFix Attack Uses Windows Terminal to Evade Detection
item.description: Fake CAPTCHA pages instruct victims to paste malicious commands in the Windows Terminal instead of the Run dialog. The post ClickFix Attack Uses Windows Terminal to Evade Detection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Internet Infrastructure TLD .arpa Abused in Phishing Attacks
item.description: Abusing DNS record management controls, the threat actor hides the location of malicious content via Cloudflare. The post Internet Infrastructure TLD .arpa Abused in Phishing Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cloned AI Tool Sites Distribute Malware in ‘InstallFix’ Campaign
item.description: Threat actors replace legitimate commands on the cloned installation webpages with malicious commands. The post Cloned AI Tool Sites Distribute Malware in ‘InstallFix’ Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Amortizing Maximum Inner Product Search with Learned Support Functions
item.description: Maximum inner product search (MIPS) is a crucial subroutine in machine learning, requiring the identification of key vectors that align best with a given query. We propose amortized MIPS: a learning-based approach that trains neural networks to directly predict MIPS solutions, amortizing the...

item.title: Incident with Codespaces
item.description: Mar 9, 03:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 9, 03:51 UTCUpdate - This incident has been resolved. New Codespace creation requests...

item.title: Hospitality-VQA: Decision-Oriented Informativeness Evaluation for Vision-Language Models
item.description: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive multimodal understanding in general domains. However, their applicability to decision-oriented domains such as hospitality remains largely unexplored. In this work, we investigate how well VLMs can perform visual question...

item.title: Ulysses Sequence Parallelism: Training with Million-Token Contexts
LeRobot v0.5.0: Scaling Every Dimension
How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these...

item.title: AWS - Europe (Zürich): INC0153183
item.description: Mar 7, 20:05 UTCInvestigating - Current status: We're investigating an issue with Snowflake Data Cloud. We'll provide an update within 60 minutes.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified regions may be unable to access or use multiple core Snowflake services and features. Affected...
